name: DNSdumpster
purpose: Web-based DNS recon — aggregates passive DNS, CT logs, BGP data, and generates a visual network graph
author: HackerTarget
why_notable: Built by Australian network security company HackerTarget around 2013, DNSdumpster's key innovation was the downloadable visual network graph at a time when every DNS tool produced only raw text. It combined passive DNS aggregation, CT log data, BGP routing tables, and MX/NS/TXT records in one view — showing hostname relationships and ASN ownership at a glance, instantly usable in a client report. It became the standard first-look tool in the 2014–2018 era before CLI tools like subfinder and amass rose to prominence. It remains relevant for quick no-setup lookups and for its uniquely useful visual output.
